title: MD
biography: Ruth Nussenzweig was a Brazilian-American parasitologist who pioneered malaria vaccine research. She was the first to demonstrate that irradiated sporozoites could protect against malaria infection in 1967, and her identification of the circumsporozoite protein laid the foundation for the RTS,S malaria vaccine (Mosquirix).
aiSummary: Ruth Nussenzweig was a parasitology and immunology specialist with an H-index of 70 at NYU School of Medicine. Pioneer of malaria vaccine research whose work led to the RTS,S vaccine. Based in New York, United States.

affiliations.hospitalName: New York University School of Medicine
affiliations.role: Professor of Medical and Molecular Parasitology

γ Interferon, CD8+ T cells and antibodies required for immunity to malaria sporozoites
TRAP Is Necessary for Gliding Motility and Infectivity of Plasmodium Sporozoites
Migration of Plasmodium Sporozoites Through Cells Before Infection
Cloned cytotoxic T cells recognize an epitope in the circumsporozoite protein and protect against malaria
Safety and immunogenicity in man of a synthetic peptide malaria vaccine against Plasmodium falciparum sporozoites
Hybridoma Produces Protective Antibodies Directed Against the Sporozoite Stage of Malaria Parasite
Monovalent fragments (Fab) of monoclonal antibodies to a sporozoite surface antigen (Pb44) protect mice against malarial infection.
Rationale for Development of a Synthetic Vaccine Against Plasmodium falciparum Malaria
Circumsporozoite protein is required for development of malaria sporozoites in mosquitoes

awards: Paul Ehrlich and Ludwig Darmstaedter Prize

timeline.title: Demonstrated irradiated sporozoites protect against malaria
timeline.description: Published landmark paper showing immunization with irradiated sporozoites prevents malaria infection

timeline.title: Identified circumsporozoite protein
timeline.description: Discovered the major surface protein of Plasmodium sporozoites

timeline.title: Cloned circumsporozoite protein gene
timeline.description: Enabled molecular development of subunit malaria vaccines

timeline.title: RTS,S vaccine approved by WHO
timeline.description: The Mosquirix malaria vaccine, built on her foundational work, received WHO recommendation
